question: What competitive threat does Douyin's shift to shelf commerce pose to Taobao and Pinduoduo?

findings.claim: Double 11 2025 category share: Apparel -- Tmall 57.5%, Douyin 26%, JD 17%. Electronics -- JD dominated (phones 56.9%, computers 62.7%)
findings.data: Double 11 2025: Apparel remained highly competitive. Tmall captured 57.5% market share, Douyin took 26%, JD secured 17%. Mobile phones: JD 56.9%. Computers: JD 62.7%. AI glasses sales jumped 25x YoY. Total Double 11 2025 GMV: RMB 1.7 trillion.

findings.claim: Douyin GMV (~3.5-4T yuan) is approaching ~44-50% the size of Taobao/Tmall (~8T yuan), growing at 30-34% vs single-digit for Taobao
findings.data: Douyin 2024 GMV: ~3.5 trillion yuan. Taotian (Taobao+Tmall) 2024 GMV: ~8 trillion yuan. Douyin GMV = ~44% of Taotian. Douyin growing at 30-34% YoY vs Taotian single-digit growth. JD.com 2024 GMV: ~4.5 trillion yuan. Douyin is third-largest e-commerce player, approaching Pinduoduo scale.

findings.claim: Douyin's strength in beauty, fashion, and impulse categories; Tmall dominates premium; JD dominates electronics
findings.data: Content platforms like Douyin are likely to gain greater share in beauty, apparel, and impulse categories. Beauty, fashion, and wellness brands particularly benefit from Douyin's creator-driven discovery path. Premium fashion, electronics, and beauty often do well on Tmall and JD. Overall market share (mid-2023): Taobao/Tmall 44%, JD 24%, Pinduoduo 19%. Apparel & footwear category held 60% of China's e-commerce market in 2024.
